questions.question: What is the standard YouTube header height?
questions.explanation: YouTube uses a 56px header height.

questions.question: How do you create a collapsible sidebar with CSS?
options: Toggle a class that changes width and hides labels
questions.explanation: Adding a 'collapsed' class that changes width and hides text labels creates the effect.

questions.question: Which CSS property creates the responsive video grid?
questions.explanation: auto-fill with minmax creates responsive video columns.

questions.question: What aspect ratio is standard for video thumbnails?
questions.explanation: 16:9 is the standard widescreen video aspect ratio.

questions.question: How do you truncate video titles to 2 lines?
questions.explanation: -webkit-line-clamp with -webkit-box-orient: vertical limits lines and adds ellipsis.

questions.question: What is a 'chip' in YouTube's UI?
questions.explanation: Chips are the horizontal scrollable filter pills below the header.

questions.question: How do you create the video duration badge?
questions.explanation: The duration badge is absolutely positioned at the bottom-right of the thumbnail.

questions.question: What CSS grid property creates the page layout?
questions.explanation: All contribute to creating the YouTube page layout with sidebar and content.

questions.question: How does the sidebar navigation work on mobile?
options: Hidden, replaced by bottom nav or hamburger
questions.explanation: YouTube hides the sidebar on mobile and uses a hamburger menu or bottom navigation.

questions.question: What ARIA attribute indicates a sidebar is collapsible?
questions.explanation: aria-expanded on the toggle button indicates sidebar state to screen readers.

questions.question: How do you create the search bar with icon inside?
options: Flexbox with input and button, or relative container with icon
questions.explanation: A flexbox row with a text input and search button, or an icon absolutely positioned inside a relative input wrapper.

questions.question: What CSS creates the video metadata layout (channel, views, date)?
questions.explanation: A flexbox column layout with small gap between channel name, views, and date is standard.

questions.question: How do you create the subscribe button styling?
options: Background color with white text and border-radius
questions.explanation: A solid background button (YouTube red #FF0000) with white text and pill shape is standard.

questions.question: What CSS creates the notification badge with count?
options: Absolute positioned span with background color and border-radius
questions.explanation: An absolutely positioned span with solid background, white text, and border-radius creates notification badges.

questions.question: How do you create the suggested videos sidebar layout?
options: Single column flexbox with horizontal video cards
questions.explanation: A single-column flex layout with thumbnail-left, info-right cards creates the suggested videos list.

questions.question: What approach creates the comments section with threaded replies?
options: Nested flexbox/cards with indentation for replies
questions.explanation: Nested flexbox containers with left margin/padding indentation for threaded replies is standard.

questions.question: How do you create the channel info section below a video?
options: Horizontal flexbox with channel avatar, name, subscriber count, and subscribe button
questions.explanation: A horizontal flexbox row with avatar, channel details column, and subscribe button is the standard pattern.

questions.question: What CSS creates the live badge indicator?
options: Red background with white text and border-radius
questions.explanation: A red pill badge with white 'LIVE' text is the standard live indicator pattern.

questions.question: What media queries are needed for a YouTube-like responsive layout?
options: Breakpoints for sidebar collapse, grid columns, and header adjustments
questions.explanation: Multiple breakpoints handle sidebar collapse, video grid column count, and mobile layout changes.

questions.question: How do you create the upload progress indicator?
options: CSS @keyframes animation on a progress bar width
questions.explanation: A CSS keyframe animation or transition on a progress bar's width creates the upload progress effect.
